title: Sutera calycina
taxonomy: Sutera calycina is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. This classification places it within the order Lamiales and the clade Asterids. Sutera calycina is native to Africa, and its classification as a member of the Scrophulariaceae family suggests a close relationship to plants in the snapdragon and foxglove families. The plant's classification is further supported by its characteristics, including its small, white flowers and hairy foliage. The exact classification of Sutera calycina at the rank of genus and species within the Scrophulariaceae family may be subject to ongoing research and refinement.

intro: Sutera calycina, also known as African boxwood or boxwood vine, is a flowering plant native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Sutera calycina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
